__4: Details

__3: Hotel Barrière Le Majestic, 10 Bd de la Croisette, 06400 Cannes
__4: The official ADWEEK House opening night reception kicking off a full week of bold debates, fresh thinking, and high-impact conversations at Hotel Barrière Le Majestic. Themed 'Creativity That Moves,' this soirée sets the tone for ADWEEK's presence at Cannes Lions 2026. Expect leading marketers, creatives, and cultural voices challenging convention over cocktails. Featured hosts include SNL cast member and Emmy-nominated comedian Bowen Yang and Olympic rugby medalist Ilona Maher. Smart casual attire; free with registration.

Early-morning networking tennis hosted by The Nucleus Network at Parc Montfleury — a relaxed, competitive way to kick off the week alongside fellow senior advertising and media professionals. Teams form on-site; all skill levels welcome. A great alternative to the packed schedule ahead, with 3 hours of play, coffee, and camaraderie before the Croisette action begins.

Cannes Lions' official mindfulness and wellness start to the week at the Palais Wellness Suite. A guided yoga session designed to ground attendees before five days of packed programming. Open to all badge holders; no advance booking required — just show up in comfortable clothing and breathe before the chaos begins.

Adobe x Lions Creator Beach @ Cannes
__3: LIONS Creators Beach, The Parvis (in front of the Palais) The Majestic Hotel, 10 Bd de la Croisette, 06400 Cannes
__4: Adobe is the official headline partner of LIONS Creators Beach at Cannes Lions 2026 — now in its third year, and relocated from the Palais rooftop to a prime beachfront location in front of the Palais and at the Majestic Hotel. The expanded Beach features a dedicated stage, content studio, editing suite, meeting areas, and sponsor activations — all centered on the business of influence. Daily programming (9AM–6PM, June 22–26) includes sessions on the creator economy, brand-creator collaboration, and AI-powered creative tools. Featured speaker: Mel Robbins. Adobe also sponsors the Young Lions Competition and hosts executive meetings at the Majestic Hotel. Free with registration; some sessions require a LIONS Creators Pass.

__3: Hotel Barrière Le Majestic, 10 Bd de la Croisette, 06400 Cannes
__4: All-day open programming at ADWEEK House (Hotel Barrière Le Majestic) as brand and media partners officially kick off their programming. Features rotating structured sessions, partner introductions, and the first wave of curated group conversations. ADWEEK House is themed 'Creativity That Moves' — a week-long hub where leading marketers and creatives turn pressure into possibility through bold debates and high-impact conversations. Free with registration.

ADWEEK House, Viant Cafe - Group Chats and Programming
__3:
Access via Viant Cafe via Hotel Barrière Le Majestic 
10 Bd de la Croisette, 06400 Cannes
__4: Casual all-day access to ADWEEK House through the Viant Café entrance at Hotel Barrière Le Majestic. Features informal group chats, smaller breakout conversations, and spontaneous networking with a rotating cast of industry guests. A less formal counterpart to the main ADWEEK House stage programming — ideal for relaxed conversations over coffee or cocktails throughout the day.

A dedicated networking session at the Palais Basement for first-time Cannes Lions attendees and junior creatives. A lower-pressure environment to meet peers, get tips for navigating the festival, and build connections before the week intensifies. Cannes Lions badge required.

Session: Turbo-charging marketing performance with AI-driven campaign intelligence
__4: Kantar's opening session at their Cannes hub (13 Rue des Serbes) dives deep into how AI-powered campaign intelligence is transforming marketing performance. Topics include AI-driven creative effectiveness scoring, real-time attribution modeling, and how brands can use live campaign intelligence to optimize mid-flight. Part of Kantar's extensive week-long programming track on creativity and effectiveness. Free with registration.

The Internet Advertising Bureau's annual standards summit, setting the global policy and technical roadmap for digital advertising in 2026/27. Key topics include cross-screen measurement harmonization, updated privacy frameworks, consent management standards, and guidance on AI-generated content in advertising. Essential for anyone involved in media policy, ad tech compliance, or programmatic standards.

Criteo's session on maximizing the retail media opportunity through Commerce Media 2.0 — covering how brands are leveraging commerce data across the open internet for personalized, performance-driven advertising at scale. Features case studies from leading retail media networks and a look at Criteo's platform evolution.

Ladies Who Strategize Annual Cannes Breakfast
__4: The annual gathering of the Ladies Who Strategize community — a tight-knit network of senior women in advertising strategy across agencies, brands, and consultancies. Held just a short walk from the Palais, this intimate breakfast features honest conversation, cross-industry networking, and the kind of candid exchange that doesn't happen in sponsored panel settings. Note: LWS events consistently sell out. Register early.

AppsFlyer's exclusive yacht-side session on privacy-first measurement in mobile advertising. Industry leaders and brand partners discuss the future of attribution in a world without third-party cookies and evolving platform privacy rules — covering incrementality testing, SKAdNetwork, and new measurement methodologies. Invite-only yacht setting; contact your AppsFlyer representative.

Meta Beach's opening session featuring a masterclass in short-form storytelling through Reels. Covers creative best practices, algorithm insights, and brand case studies on winning with vertical video. Meta's creative strategists lead hands-on creative workshops. Meta Beach is one of the festival's most prominent activations; this session is invite-only — contact your Meta representative for access.

Workday's session on building authentic brand equity from the inside out — exploring how employee experience, organizational culture, and internal brand alignment create stronger external marketing. Panels feature senior HR and marketing leaders from global brands. Invite-only.

MNTN's yacht programming focuses on democratizing connected TV advertising for brands of all sizes — not just enterprise budgets. Sessions cover CTV creative strategy, how MNTN's self-serve platform lowers the barrier to streaming, and measurement frameworks for proving CTV performance. Invite-only.

A strategic yacht lunch for travel and hospitality marketing leaders. Sojern presents data-driven insights specific to the travel vertical — covering booking funnel optimization, destination marketing, audience targeting across digital channels, and recovery signals in global travel demand. Invite-only.

Reddit's community-focused lunch explores the power of engaged, niche online communities in the age of AI. Sessions cover how brands authentically participate in community spaces, the value of user-generated content, and Reddit's evolving advertising ecosystem — including AI-adjacent conversation trends and r/ community brand integration strategies. Invite-only at Reddit Beach.

6Sense's yacht session on AI-powered revenue intelligence for B2B marketers. Topics cover predictive analytics, intent data, account-based marketing (ABM), and how 6Sense's Revenue AI platform helps B2B go-to-market teams identify in-market accounts and personalize outreach at scale. Invite-only.

__3:
Fouquet’s, Hôtel Barrière Le Majestic
10 Bd de la Croisette, 06400 Cannes
__4: Limelight Inc.'s exclusive invite-only networking lunch at Fouquet's in Hotel Barrière Le Majestic — a high-end gathering of adtech founders, investors, and senior marketing technology leaders to kick off the week's adtech conversation. Intimate format; curated guest list. Invite-only through Limelight Inc.

A focused festival workshop exploring how AI tools are being deployed for social impact across climate, education, healthcare, and social equity by brands, NGOs, and nonprofits. An honest assessment of AI's potential for positive change alongside its risks. Part of Cannes Lions' broader purpose programming track. Cannes Lions badge required.

Oracle Advertising's panel discussion on navigating the post-cookie landscape, featuring practical guidance on first-party data strategy, data clean rooms, identity resolution without third-party cookies, and what the deprecation of traditional targeting means for large-scale marketers. Invite-only.

Session on performance marketing powered by machine learning algorithms. Moloco's team and brand partners discuss how ML-driven advertising delivers privacy-safe personalization and real-time optimization across mobile, streaming, and commerce environments — without relying on personal identifiers. Invite-only.

OneTrust's session on building consumer trust through ethical data practices and privacy-by-design principles. Covers GDPR, global privacy compliance frameworks, consent management, and how privacy-first brands build long-term equity. Invite-only.

Emodo's session on using AI for real-time creative optimization — how their platform dynamically adapts ad creative to context, audience signals, and live performance data, enabling personalized creative at scale without manual production overhead.

Anzu's session on blending advertising seamlessly into gaming environments. Covers non-intrusive in-game ad formats (banner ads on in-game billboards, digital signage in virtual spaces) that respect player experience while delivering strong brand impact across mobile, PC, and console. Features case studies from global gaming campaigns. Invite-only.

Session: Creative consequences: why the world’s strongest brands create the world's best ads
__4: Kantar's landmark session presenting hard data linking creative quality directly to business outcomes. Demonstrates how the world's highest-scoring brands on Kantar's Brand Power metric consistently produce the most creatively excellent and commercially effective advertising — and why the false trade-off between creativity and effectiveness is a myth. Features global case studies from award-winning campaigns with effectiveness metrics to match. Free with registration.

Taboola's yacht programming on the future of the open web and content discovery beyond the social media walled gardens. Covers native advertising best practices, editorial partnership models, Taboola's AI-driven recommendation engine, and the commercial opportunity in reaching audiences outside of Google and Meta. Invite-only.

Liftoff's afternoon session on performance marketing strategies for mobile app growth. Covers user acquisition, retargeting, creative optimization, ROAS measurement, and privacy-compliant attribution for app marketers navigating a post-IDFA ecosystem. Invite-only.

DoubleVerify presents its latest research on brand safety and verification in connected TV environments — covering how advertisers can protect brand integrity across the rapidly expanding streaming landscape, including fraud detection in CTV, viewability standards, and contextual alignment. Afternoon tea networking included. Invite-only.

PubMatic's sunset yacht mixer bringing together publishers, DSPs, and programmatic ecosystem leaders for an evening of cocktails and conversation about open-market programmatic yield optimization, private marketplace strategy, and the shifting balance between walled gardens and the open web. Invite-only.

Vice's sunset yacht session marking the launch of their annual cultural trends report — packed with Gen Z and millennial consumer behavior insights and cultural predictions for the year ahead. Features a drinks reception as the sun sets over the Mediterranean with senior media and brand marketers. Invite-only.

The Trade Desk: Media for Good
__4: The Trade Desk's yacht session on how premium programmatic investment can actively support quality journalism, fight disinformation, and raise industry standards. Covers TTD's Seller-Defined Audiences framework and the case for premium open-internet inventory. Features a sunset drinks reception. Invite-only.

Campaign US celebrates its annual 40 Over 40 honorees — experienced creative and marketing leaders recognized for sustained career impact and industry leadership — at a dedicated reception at the Campaign US Hub. An opportunity to network with the festival's most seasoned professionals. Free with registration.

__3: Hotel Barrière Le Majestic, 10 Bd de la Croisette, 06400 Cannes
__4: ADWEEK and Adobe's joint sunset reception at ADWEEK House (Hotel Barrière Le Majestic), featuring structured conversations on AI-powered creative tools, Adobe's latest innovations in GenStudio and Creative Cloud, and the future of the creative workflow. Live product demos and networking over cocktails. Free with registration.

Session: Inside the sustainability leaders playbook for brand growth
__4: Kantar presents research on how sustainability-led brands systematically outperform peers on long-term brand equity and revenue growth. Features practical frameworks for integrating sustainability into brand strategy and measuring the commercial return of purpose-led marketing. Free with registration.

The List Cannes Kickoff - Ad Age
__4: Ad Age kicks off Cannes Lions week with the official opening celebration for The List — a curated who's-who of marketing's annual top honorees. Industry leaders, senior brand marketers, and advertising professionals gather for cocktails and networking to set the tone for the week ahead. Free with registration.

Publicis Groupe: The Power of One Mixer
__4: Publicis Groupe's internal networking reception bringing together global leadership teams from across the Publicis network — including Publicis, Saatchi & Saatchi, Leo Burnett, BBH, and Publicis Media — to align on the week and connect with key clients. A central touchpoint for the Publicis organization at Cannes. Invite-only.

Microsoft's opening evening happy hour at 64 Bd de la Croisette (Microsoft Gardens). Features conversations on Microsoft Advertising's AI-powered platform capabilities, Copilot integrations for marketers, and the future of search and native advertising — all over casual cocktails. Free with registration.

__3: LBB & Friends Beach, Boulevard de la Croisette (Opposite Hotel Miramar)
__4: The Marketing Society hosts its 'Connections and Conversations' kickoff evening at LBB & Friends Beach. Designed to help members orient themselves for the week, the event features informal roundtables, light programming, and the welcoming LBB Beach atmosphere. A great entry point into the Cannes Lions week for Marketing Society members and their guests. Free with registration.

Cannes 2026 Event Calander: LBB x Bear x Revolver x Rumble x ACA
__4: The annual Australian advertising and production community welcome drinks, jointly hosted by LBB, Bear, Revolver, Rumble, and the ACA at LBB & Friends Beach. A beloved tradition bringing together the Australian and New Zealand creative industry diaspora for their first collective gathering of the week. Relaxed, fun, and a great way to find your people on Day 1.

The official opening night of the 2026 Cannes Lions International Festival of Creativity at Carlton Beach. Black tie optional. Brings together festival badge holders for the ceremonial launch of the week — expect remarks from festival leadership, the first Lion awards of the week, and a lively celebration on the beach to kick off five days of creative excellence. One of the defining Cannes Lions experiences; Cannes Lions badge required.

Digiday's exclusive, invite-only dinner at Le Cirque for brand-side marketing leaders only. An intimate gathering of CMOs and senior brand marketers for candid, off-the-record conversation about the state of marketing in 2026. No agencies, no pitches — just genuine peer discussion among the most senior brand voices at the festival. One of the most sought-after dinner invitations of the week.

Digital Fight Club's high-energy opening night bash at the Palais de la Méditerranée, featuring a live performance from a special musical guest and cocktail-fueled networking. Digital Fight Club is the legendary Cannes event where top agency CEOs spar on stage in front of CMOs and 300 industry peers armed with cocktails and encouraged to cheer and heckle — 'harder than winning a Lions.' This afterparty is the post-fight celebration. Free with registration.

Spotify Beach opens its signature nighttime concert programming on Monday, transforming the beachfront into a world-class music venue. Expect live performances from emerging global artists — in past years performers have included Alex Warren, Cardi B, Mark Ronson, Royel Otis, and others — plus surprise DJ sets by Baby J and Dre Tala. Spotify Beach is consistently one of the most talked-about nightlife events of the entire festival. By day, the Beach hosts conversations with musicians, podcasters, and brand leaders. Invite-only; contact your Spotify representative to register.

MGM's exclusive private villa party for senior industry leaders — one of the most coveted invitations of opening night. Features high-energy entertainment, premium open bar, and a curated guest list of industry leaders and entertainment figures. Location kept private until the day of. Invite-only.

Breakfast and Programming: Commerce Connections presented by SMG
__3:
Hotel Barrière Le Majestic
10 Bd de la Croisette, 06400 Cannes
__4: SMG and ADWEEK team up for a morning of commerce media programming at ADWEEK House (Hotel Barrière Le Majestic). Sessions explore the biggest opportunities in commerce media today — from the technology powering retail media networks to how in-store media is redefining the in-store customer experience. Includes full breakfast service. Free with registration.

An executive-level roundtable aboard the Teads yacht, designed as a small and curated C-suite gathering focused on the future of media and the open internet. Covers Teads' Instream format innovations, contextual advertising research, and the premium publisher ecosystem. Invite-only.

Ad Age's flagship invite-only Tuesday morning gathering for chief marketing officers. An intimate conversation at 57 Bd de la Croisette on the biggest strategic challenges and opportunities facing brand marketing leaders in 2026 — including AI integration, creator economy investment, measurement standards, and organizational change. Limited attendance ensures genuinely candid exchanges. Invite-only.

A dedicated breakfast hosted by Ad Age and Adlatina for members of the Leading Women community at Cannes Lions. Brings together senior women in marketing from North and Latin America for structured conversation on leadership, career development, and cross-cultural brand strategy. Open to registered attendees.

Amazon Ads' flagship Cannes Lions activation, inspired by French fashion houses and themed around 'connecting customers wherever they spend their time.' The sprawling Amazon Port features: daily wellness sessions (Mon/Wed/Thu), industry talks with brands including AdCouncil and Accenture Song, a rosé and iced coffee fountain, Prime Video slushie bar, AWS-inspired custom perfume bar, and mechanical fortune teller for Amazon Devices. Programming covers full-funnel commerce advertising, AI-powered audience targeting using Amazon's authenticated graph of 300M+ US households, and the Prime Video advertising ecosystem. Open daily Tuesday–Friday, 9AM–5PM. Free with registration.

Marketing Dive's breakfast panel at the InterContinental, featuring senior marketing and retail media executives discussing the evolving relationship between brands, retailers, publishers, and platform ecosystems. Invite-only.

Peloton starts Tuesday morning with a guided workout session at Spotify Beach, followed by a panel on how Peloton and other fitness brands integrate content into their broader marketing strategy. Covers community-led brand building, subscription media models, and the growth of content-led brand ecosystems. Invite-only.

ADWEEK House Open Doors, Viant Cafe - Group Chats and Programming
__3:
Access to Viant Cafe via Hotel Barrière Le Majestic Cannes
10 Bd de la Croisette, 06400 Cannes
__4: Continued Tuesday all-day ADWEEK House programming through the Viant Café entrance at Hotel Barrière Le Majestic. Accessible networking and informal group conversations with industry guests throughout the day. Free with registration.

Session: Creativity is business: Europe's most effective advertising
__4: Kantar presents research-led analysis of European advertising effectiveness, comparing the creative quality and commercial performance of campaigns across the UK, Germany, France, and beyond. Features award-winning campaigns alongside effectiveness data to demonstrate the direct link between creative excellence and sales outcomes. Free with registration.

Pinterest's award-winning immersive brand activation — consistently voted the most interactive experience on the Croisette — now in its fourth year at Cannes Lions. The 2026 Manifestival brings Pinterest Predicts™ trend experiences to life through hands-on activities: the returning Pinterest Tattoo Parlour (themed around trend colors Cool Blue, Gimme Gummy, and Cabbage Crush), artist-led beauty bars with L'Oréal Paris, collage and art workshops, style readings using Pinterest visual search, and pop-up DIY experiences. No boring panels — only doing. Gen Z attendees and brand marketers alike discover why Pinterest is 'The Manifestation App.' Open 10AM–6PM Tuesday at Pinterest Beach.

Official Cannes Lions portfolio review sessions for junior creatives, held in the Palais Foyer. Attendees can have their portfolio reviewed by senior industry professionals and jury members for direct feedback and career guidance. An invaluable development opportunity for those early in their creative careers. Cannes Lions badge required.

GroupM's major industry keynote presenting the latest edition of 'This Year Next Year' — the world's most closely followed global media investment forecast. Covers total global ad spend, the rise of retail media, CTV investment trends, AI's impact on media planning, and regional market breakdowns. One of the most data-rich and widely cited briefings of the entire festival. Invite-only.

DoubleVerify: The State of Brand Safety
__4: DoubleVerify presents new global research on disinformation, ad fraud, and brand safety across digital channels in 2026. Covers emerging threats in connected TV, social media, and AI-generated content environments — plus DV's roadmap for the next generation of verification technology. Invite-only.

Digiday's invite-only Tuesday brunch gathering for senior marketing leaders at Medami restaurant (30 bis Rue Hoche), featuring intimate off-the-record discussion on the week's biggest themes in media and marketing. Invite-only.

Yahoo! Beach: Back to the Future
__4: Yahoo Beach's full-day Tuesday activation (11AM–5PM) with a nostalgic-meets-future-forward theme. Programming covers Yahoo's evolved advertising platform (DSP, native, and mail), creator partnerships, and what the open web renaissance means for brand marketers — all with a playful throwback aesthetic. Features sessions, networking, and refreshments throughout the day. Invite-only.

Outfront's yacht session on the digital transformation of out-of-home advertising. Covers programmatic DOOH (digital out-of-home), data-driven audience targeting in physical spaces, measurement advancements for OOH, and real-world case studies of brands winning in the physical world. Invite-only.

Twitch's programming on reaching and engaging the live-streaming generation. Features conversations on how brands build authentic relationships with gaming and streaming communities, live-streaming sponsorship formats, creator partnership models, and Twitch's advertising solutions. Invite-only.

Stagwell's Villa session on efficiency and creativity at scale. Covers the Stagwell Marketing Cloud platform, integrated data and creative tools, and how Stagwell agencies (72andSunny, Anomaly, Assembly, GALE, Code and Theory, and others) build modern marketing operations. Also related to SPORT BEACH programming. Free with registration.

WPP: The Future of AI in Creative
__4: WPP's session on AI-powered creativity, now hosted at the WPP Rooftop at the Martinez Hotel (WPP gave up its traditional beach space in 2026). Features WPP agency leaders alongside NVIDIA exploring how generative AI is transforming creative production, brand storytelling, and campaign optimization. WPP's 2026 Cannes presence focuses on invitation-only, client-priority engagements — including the WPP Rooftop lounge (all-day hospitality and interactive demos) and a curated programme of conversations. Free/invite only depending on session.

WBD's invitation-only first-look presentation of the 2026/27 film and TV slate for advertising partners. Includes a preview of upcoming releases from Warner Bros. Pictures, HBO, Max, Discovery, and CNN — along with WBD's advertising partnership opportunities across the full portfolio. Invite-only.

__3: FT x Nikkei Teahouse, 11 Rue de Belges, 06400 Cannes
__4: The Financial Times and Ad Council co-host an invitation-only brunch briefing at the FT x Nikkei Teahouse (11 Rue des Belges). Features editorial briefings from senior FT journalists on global business, media investment, and marketing trends, followed by a networking reception for senior executives attending the festival. Invite-only.

GumGum's yacht session on the renaissance of contextual advertising powered by AI computer vision. Covers how GumGum's Verity™ technology understands page and video content at a granular level to enable hyper-relevant ad placement — without personal data. Features the latest contextual effectiveness research. Free with registration.

Session: Creative ReCode: how to sell to humans and algorithms
__4: Kantar's session on what creative effectiveness looks like in an era where both human audiences and algorithmic content feeds must be satisfied simultaneously. Research-driven insights on creative formats that win in the attention economy while also performing in platform optimization environments. Free with registration.

Fortune's high-impact invitation-only gathering at 38 Rue des Serbes — one of Cannes' most prestigious power lunches. Brings together the festival's most influential CMOs for editorially-led panel discussions with Fortune's global editors, VIP networking, and an intimate lunch format. Previous editions have featured Fortune 500 CMOs sharing unfiltered perspectives on AI, growth, and creativity. Topics are set by Fortune's editorial team, not sponsors. Invite-only.

Bloomberg Media's invitation-only lunch for senior marketing and media executives, featuring briefings from Bloomberg journalists on the global economic outlook and its implications for media investment, brand strategy, and advertising markets. Invite-only.

A small, high-quality invitation-only lunch hosted by industry writer and creative advocate David Griner, focused on creative mentorship, career development, and leadership development for the next generation of creative leaders. An intimate gathering with genuine conversation — not a panel format. Invite-only.

Magnite's strategic yacht lunch for global publishers and SSP partners, covering the future of programmatic supply — including CTV supply quality, supply path optimization, publisher monetization strategies, and the evolving publisher-advertiser relationship. Free/invite depending on access level.

The Marketing, Content & Commerce Leadership Lunch
__4: An intimate lunch at La Mome (6 Rue Florian) organized by Martech Record, bringing together commerce, content, and marketing leaders for a candid roundtable on how commerce is reshaping content strategy, monetization, and the future of the marketing funnel. Free with registration.

The New York Times: Truth in Advertising
__4: The New York Times presents a panel on brand safety and the commercial value of trusted journalism environments for advertisers. Covers the link between editorial quality and brand perception, the Times' advertising solutions, and the broader industry conversation about where brands choose to place their dollars. Invite-only.

Smartly's yacht session on automating ad creative production and optimization across social platforms. Features product demonstrations of Smartly's AI-powered creative tools and brand case studies on scaling social creative without sacrificing quality or brand consistency. Free with registration.

Teads presents a green media panel on sustainability in digital advertising — covering carbon measurement of digital campaigns, sustainable media buying practices, publisher responsibilities, and the industry's collective path toward net zero advertising. Invite-only.

Basis Technologies presents their vision for automation-first media operations — covering the role of AI in programmatic workflows, automated campaign management, and how media teams can reclaim time through intelligent workflow automation while maintaining strategic oversight. Invite-only.

Panel: The creative power of sports
__4: Kantar's panel on the commercial effectiveness of sports sponsorship and sports-themed creative. Presents data on the return on investment of major sports marketing and sponsorship, with case studies from brands winning in the sports marketing arena. Particularly relevant given LIONS Sport's inaugural launch this year. Free with registration.

Seedtag's yacht session on visual AI and the future of contextual advertising. Covers how Seedtag's Liz™ AI engine understands images and videos at a contextual level to deliver highly relevant, non-intrusive advertising — and the brand safety and performance advantages of context-first targeting. Invite-only.

Cannes Panel & Cocktail Reception - Brand, agency and publishing executives can apply to attend
__4: Digiday's Tuesday afternoon panel featuring brand, agency, and publishing executives discussing the most pressing issues in digital media and marketing — applied directly to the themes emerging at Cannes Lions this year. Followed by a cocktail reception open to approved senior attendees. Brand, agency, and publishing executives can apply to attend at digiday.com. Invite-only through application.

Exclusive Briefing & Networking Reception evening - FT x People Like Us
__3: FT x Nikkei Teahouse, 11 Rue de Belges, 06400, Cannes
__4: The Financial Times partners with People Like Us — a community championing diversity and inclusion in the creative industries — for an evening briefing and networking reception at the FT x Nikkei Teahouse. Features editorial discussion on representation in marketing, media, and creative leadership, followed by networking over cocktails. Invite-only.

The Cannes Social - Networking and drinks
__4: A popular, informal drinks gathering at Ma Nolan's Irish pub (6 Rue Buttura), organized by ExchangeWire and FirstPartyCapital. Open to adtech, martech, and media professionals across the industry for relaxed, agenda-free networking. A beloved low-key counterpoint to the week's more formal programming. Free with registration.

The Drum's legendary annual pub quiz — consistently one of the most competitive and entertaining events of the Cannes week. Teams from agencies, platforms, and brands battle it out with industry-specific questions, creative trivia, and more. No formal networking agenda; just fun, fierce competition, and drinks. Arrive early to secure your table. Free with registration.

Networking reception for the open-source header bidding and programmatic technology community — including adtech developers, publishers, and demand partners committed to maintaining an independent, transparent programmatic ecosystem. Invite-only.

Kantar's closing Tuesday session on dynamic creative intelligence — how real-time data and AI can inform creative decisions throughout a campaign's lifecycle, from pre-testing through mid-flight optimization to post-campaign learning. Free with registration.

Ad Age's dedicated gathering for independent and smaller agency leaders at 57 Bd de la Croisette — a rare space within Cannes for smaller shops to connect, share challenges, and network in an environment that can otherwise feel dominated by holding companies. Free with registration.

Telemundo's invitation-only evening event at their yacht celebrating Hispanic culture, talent, and the commercial power of multicultural media. Features entertainment, networking, and a showcase of Telemundo's advertising solutions and multicultural marketing research. Invite-only.

INDVSTRY CLVB's community dinner in Antibes — a warm and intentional gathering celebrating diversity and inclusion within the global creative industry. Brings together multicultural marketing leaders and industry professionals for a shared meal and genuine conversation outside the festival bubble. Free with registration.

Beet.tv celebrates its 20th anniversary at Cannes Lions at Yahoo Beach, marking two decades as the leading video journalism platform for the digital media and advertising industry. The event features tributes to industry leaders and a retrospective on how streaming, CTV, and digital video transformed advertising over 20 years — alongside toasts to what's next. Free with registration.

Pinterest extends its Manifestival into a lively nighttime beach party, continuing the creative and playful spirit of the daytime activation. Features celebrity DJ sets, immersive Pinterest-branded experiences, and one of the most colorful, energetic parties of Tuesday night. Free/invite only.

AU Après Dark - drinks, a live DJ set, and a break from all the panels
__3: Glass Club: 6 Rue des Frères Pradignac, 06400 Cannes
__4: Adelaide Metrics' evening gathering at Glass Club (6 Rue des Frères Pradignac) — a live DJ set, drinks, and genuine decompression from the day's panels. Explicitly promises no agendas and no presentations, just relaxed networking for the digital media and measurement community. Free with registration.

Pinterest's late-night continuation of Tuesday's Manifestival energy at Pinterest Beach — featuring DJ sets, immersive Pinterest-branded experiences, and one of the festival's most colorful and vibrant nighttime parties. A fan favorite for creatives. Free/invite only.

__3: Marea Rooftop, 2 Bd Jean Hibert, 06400 Cannes
__4: VideoWeek Live hosts a live podcast recording and leadership lunch at Marea Rooftop (2 Bd Jean Hibert), billing itself as the CTV networking highlight of the week. Brings together streaming, CTV, and video advertising leaders for on-record discussion and beachside networking. Free with registration.

The official Cannes Lions charity 5K run along the Croisette at sunrise, open to all badge holders. A community wellness tradition raising money for a nominated charitable cause. A popular way to start Wednesday feeling accomplished before the day's programming begins. Cannes Lions badge required.

GWI's invitation-only breakfast session presenting their deep-dive into the 2026 global consumer mindset — covering values, media habits, brand trust levels, AI adoption behavior, and purchasing patterns across key global markets. One of the most data-rich consumer intelligence briefings of the week. Invite-only.

MNTN's Wednesday morning session specifically on how brands are successfully scaling in connected TV via self-serve advertising. Covers CTV creative best practices, measurement frameworks, and how MNTN's platform is opening premium streaming inventory to brands previously priced out. Invite-only.

Ad Age editor Tim Nudd leads a must-attend morning conversation reviewing the year's strongest creative contenders and making Grand Prix predictions with a panel of creative industry leaders. A tradition for anyone closely following the awards. Free with registration.

Session: The street is the strategy: why context beats platforms
__4: Kantar's session presenting evidence that contextual advertising — reaching people in the right environment — consistently drives stronger brand effectiveness than platform-based behavioral targeting. Backed by cross-market effectiveness data from Kantar's global research. Free with registration.

Google's major all-day activation (10AM–7PM) at the Google Pier, featuring interactive AI experiments, live demonstrations of Google's latest AI tools (including Gemini integrations and DeepMind research applications), product showcases, and a packed programme of sessions on the future of AI-powered creativity, media, and commerce. One of the festival's top destinations for hands-on AI exploration. Invite-only.

Meta Beach's Wednesday programming focuses on the future of Reels, AI-enhanced social content creation, and Meta's latest advertising innovations across Facebook, Instagram, Threads, and WhatsApp. Full-day sessions (10AM–6PM) with brand case studies, product demos, and creator conversations. Invite-only.

Gary Vaynerchuk leads a session at Vayner Beach on the attention economy and the art of social-first brand strategy. Covers how brands win in short-form content environments, the importance of organic and paid social integration, and why human storytelling is more critical than ever in an AI-saturated landscape. Invite-only.

The Ad Council's Palais Mainstage panel on social impact marketing in 2026 — featuring case studies from major public service campaigns and a discussion on how purpose-driven creative earns genuine cultural relevance (not just brand credit). Covers how major brands are using creativity to address real societal issues. Invite-only.

Amazon Ads' flagship Wednesday deep-dive into full-funnel commerce advertising at Amazon Port. Covers Prime Video advertising, the Amazon DSP's authenticated audience reach (300M+ US households), Twitch integration, AI-powered creative tools, and how brands connect with Amazon customers across the full purchase journey. Hands-on demos available throughout the day. Free with registration.

Lotame's session on identity strategies for a cookieless world — covering their Panorama ID solution, the landscape of privacy-compliant addressability, and how publishers and advertisers can maintain scale and relevance without relying on third-party identifiers. Free/invite depending on access.

WOMEN IN AI: Designing a Safer, Smarter Future
Cannes 2026 Event Calander: The Digital Marketeer Group | Human Security
__4: A morning session hosted by The Digital Marketeer Group and Human Security on women's leadership in AI and ethical AI design. Covers representation gaps in AI development teams, how diverse perspectives drive safer and fairer AI systems, and practical steps brands can take to advance responsible AI adoption. Free with registration.

Snap's all-day activation (11AM–5PM) at La Malmaison showcasing the cutting edge of AR technology — featuring live demos of the latest Snap Spectacles, augmented reality lenses for commerce and entertainment, and Snap's suite of AR tools for brands. A hands-on creative playground for anyone interested in the future of spatial computing and immersive brand experiences. Invite-only.

Index Exchange's session on the future of the programmatic supply chain — covering supply path optimization (SPO), CTV supply quality standards, transparency initiatives, and IX's global publisher partnership ecosystem. Invite-only.

Snap's focused session on how augmented reality is driving measurable retail conversion. Features brand case studies of Snap's virtual try-on tools and AR shopping experiences, live product demos, and research on how AR engagement translates to purchase intent. Invite-only.

Exclusive brunch: FT Morning Insights - The Business of Influence
__3: FT x Nikkei Teahouse, 11 Rue de Belges, 06400 Cannes
__4: The FT hosts an invitation-only morning insights brunch at the FT x Nikkei Teahouse exploring how media, influence, and trust intersect in modern marketing. Features editorial analysis from FT journalists alongside CMO and brand leader perspectives. Invite-only.

Accenture Song's session on transformation through design and technology — exploring how experience-led brand strategy, design thinking, and connected customer journeys drive long-term business performance. Covers Accenture Song's work across brand, product, and service experience. Invite-only.

Equativ's yacht session on direct-to-consumer ad tech strategies and the future of the publisher-advertiser relationship — covering how publishers are reclaiming control of their inventory and audience data through direct-deal structures and first-party data monetization. Free with registration.

Teads presents a strategy and research session on integrating connected TV into global media plans — covering supply quality, brand safety in streaming, measurement standards, and how Teads' global premium publisher network is expanding into the CTV ecosystem. Invite-only.

Integral Ad Science's yacht lunch on media quality, viewability, and transparency standards in global advertising. A curated gathering for senior media buyers and publishers discussing how quality metrics are evolving in the face of CTV expansion and AI-generated content. Invite-only.

OpenX's session on reducing the environmental footprint of digital advertising — covering OpenX's carbon-neutral auction commitments, their partnership with carbon measurement organizations, and the industry's collective path toward more sustainable programmatic operations. Invite-only.

Session: Creator-led marketing: from chaos to catalyst
__4: Kantar presents effectiveness data comparing creator-led marketing campaigns to traditional paid media — covering reach, brand metric lift, conversion, and how to structure creator partnerships for maximum commercial impact. Free with registration.

The Ritualicious Lunch with The Marketing Society & MSQ (Invite only)
__3: Le Club MSQ at Fervor, 5 Rue des Frères Casanova, 06400, Cannes
__4: An invitation-only lunch at Le Club MSQ at Fervor (5 Rue des Frères Casanova), hosted by MSQ and The Marketing Society. Described as insight 'served at scale' — brings together senior marketers from The Marketing Society community and MSQ partner agencies for a curated discussion alongside a fine dining experience. Invite-only.

Impact.com's yacht session on the rise of influencer and affiliate marketing as strategic partnership channels. Covers how brands are building scalable, trackable partnership programs, measuring full-funnel impact, and why the partnership economy is the fastest-growing segment of performance marketing. Invite-only.

The Hollywood Reporter's invitation-only power lunch at the Carlton Terrace — the intersection of celebrity, entertainment, and brand marketing. Features senior executives from studios, talent agencies, brands, and media companies discussing entertainment-driven marketing and the value of celebrity-brand partnerships. Invite-only.

Vevo's yacht session on the cultural and commercial power of music videos as brand marketing platforms. Covers how music videos drive streaming engagement, cultural conversation, and brand awareness — and Vevo's advertising solutions for reaching music fans at moments of genuine emotion and attention. Invite-only.

Microsoft and Xbox's session at the Microsoft Hub on the future of advertising in gaming environments. Covers Xbox advertising solutions, Game Pass audience data, the demographics and spending behavior of today's gamers, and the growing brand opportunity in gaming — from in-game placements to creator partnerships. Invite-only.

Smart AdServer's session on publisher yield optimization strategies — covering header bidding, private marketplace structuring, dynamic floor pricing, and how publishers can maximize revenue in a complex, multi-channel supply landscape. Invite-only.

Outbrain's yacht session on driving content discovery and engagement on the open web beyond social platforms. Covers native advertising performance data, the role of recommendation-driven content marketing, and Outbrain's AI-powered targeting capabilities for reaching high-intent audiences on premium publisher sites. Invite-only.

Variety's suite session exploring the blurring lines between Hollywood entertainment and brand marketing. Features editorial discussion on the year's most notable brand-entertainment collaborations, branded content, and the evolving relationship between studios and advertisers. Invite-only.

AppsFlyer's afternoon session on real-time mobile measurement and ROI calculation in a privacy-first world. Covers incrementality testing, predictive analytics, privacy-compliant attribution frameworks, and how mobile marketers can prove business impact without compromising user trust. Invite-only.

Session: Made in Germany 3.0: AI meets creative reality
__4: Kantar's session on how German brands are integrating AI into their creative strategies and what that means for advertising effectiveness. Features German market case studies and cross-market comparison data. Free with registration.

The Muse by Clio: Ads of the Week Live
__4: Amy Corr hosts a live, on-stage breakdown of the festival week's strongest and most talked-about creative work at the Clio Hub. Features real-time commentary from senior creative judges, audience voting, spirited debate about Lion-worthy work, and a celebration of the boldest ideas from across the week. Free with registration.

Quantcast's session on understanding the cookieless consumer — covering Quantcast's AI-powered audience modeling, real-time bid decisioning, and how marketers can maintain addressability and scale in a world without third-party identifiers. Invite-only.

__3: Duke’s Bar 59 Rue Felix Faure 06400 Cannes (Maison NDA)
__4: Publisher Perspectives' invitation-only Cannes gathering at Duke's Bar / Maison NDA (59 Rue Felix Faure) — a carefully curated C-suite publisher event for senior media leaders and industry decision-makers. Off-the-record, no hard sell — just genuine conversation for the publisher community over drinks. Reach out to Publisher Perspectives directly for an invitation. Free with registration (invite-only).

Adweek's mid-week community garden party at Hotel Barrière Le Majestic, open to ADWEEK House badge holders and guests. Features networking with Adweek editors, brand partners, and creative industry leaders in a relaxed outdoor setting. Free with registration.

Adform's session making the case for a transparent, independent ad tech stack — free from the structural conflicts of walled-garden platforms. Covers enterprise ad server technology, data sovereignty, privacy-by-design infrastructure, and why brand safety requires platform independence. Invite-only.

A friendly agency beach volleyball competition on Plage Mace organized by agency teams attending the festival. Teams sign up on the day; a great chance to decompress from the week's intensity with some sand, sun, and healthy competition. Free; open registration on the day.

Global's cocktail reception and panel on the commercial power of audio advertising — covering podcasting, DAB radio, streaming audio, and Global's UK and European audio portfolio. Features discussions on the measurable impact of audio brand investment and the growing case for audio in the media mix. Invite-only.

__3: The Terrace, Barrel Pub - 17 Rue Gérard Monod
__4: Covatic hosts a genuinely relaxed cocktail hour at the Barrel Pub terrace (17 Rue Gérard Monod). Explicitly no agendas, no presentations — just a friendly space for peers in the digital media and data industry to connect over drinks before the evening begins. Perfect for a wind-down between afternoon sessions and the night's parties. Free with registration.

__3: The Duke's 59, Rue Félix Faure Cannes
__4: Affiliate and commerce marketing leaders from across the industry gather for cocktails at The Duke's (59 Rue Félix Faure) ahead of dinner — jointly hosted by Martech Record, ShopBack, and Rakuten Advertising. A targeted gathering for the performance and affiliate marketing community. Free with registration.

Last Night a DJ Saved My Pitch - A live DJ set. A live talk
__3: Fervor, 5 Rue des Frères Casanova, 06400 Cannes
__4: MSQ's unique Wednesday evening event at Le Club MSQ (Fervor, 5 Rue des Frères Casanova) — an unorthodox blend of live DJ set and live industry talk. Features both a DJ performance and a speaker conversation, creating an atmosphere that's half networking event, half music experience. Free with registration.

Session: Creativity is business: France's most effective advertising
__4: Kantar's French-language session presenting research on the most effective advertising in France — with local market insights and French campaign case studies demonstrating the link between creative quality and commercial effectiveness. Note: this session is delivered in French. Free with registration.

An informal cocktail reception for Sizmek's ad server clients and partners, with conversation on the latest developments in ad tech, ad serving technology, and programmatic campaign management. Invite-only.

__3: The Lawn at Mondrian, 45 Bd de la Croisette, 06400 Cannes
__4: Ad Age's popular open-air gathering at the Mondrian Hotel lawn (45 Bd de la Croisette) — an inclusive, festive networking event open to the broader Ad Age community. Features complimentary cocktails, an outdoor concert-like atmosphere, and the kind of relaxed mingling that can yield better connections than any formal reception. Free with registration.

Cocktails and Conversations - ADWEEK House Creative 100 Reception
__3: Hotel Barrière Le Majestic, 10 Bd de la Croisette, 06400 Cannes
__4: ADWEEK House's exclusive reception celebrating ADWEEK's annual Creative 100 list — recognizing the most creative, innovative people in marketing and media. Features the honorees, senior members of the selection committee, and a curated invite-only guest list of creative industry leaders. Invite-only through ADWEEK.

Adweek's flagship evening gala at the Majestic Hotel honoring the Brand Stars — ADWEEK's annual award recognizing CMOs and brand marketers who have driven exceptional creative and commercial performance. One of the week's premier formal evening events, combining an awards ceremony with a gala reception. Free with registration (limited capacity).

WBD's exclusive premiere screening event at Carlton Beach — featuring a first-look screening of content from Warner Bros., HBO, or Max, followed by a private industry reception. One of the festival's most glamorous invite-only events. Invite-only.

The official Cannes Lions mid-week celebration at Carlton Beach, open to all festival badge holders. Marks the midpoint of the 2026 festival with DJ sets, cocktails, and a celebration of the week's creative work so far. A must-attend Cannes Lions tradition. Badge required.

TikTok's second night of entertainment at the TikTok Garden, featuring live musical performances and immersive content creation activations. TikTok's parties consistently bring some of the most energetic nightlife of the festival — reflecting the platform's own creator energy and cultural exuberance. Invite-only.

Spotify Beach's Wednesday night concert featuring a global superstar headliner. Past Spotify Beach performers have included Cardi B, Mark Ronson, Alex Warren, Royel Otis, and others — and surprise performances are a Spotify tradition. The concert transforms the beachfront into a world-class outdoor venue from 9PM. Invite-only; contact your Spotify representative.

WPP's signature Wednesday night party — now hosted at the WPP Rooftop at the Martinez Hotel or a private venue following WPP's decision to give up its traditional beach space in 2026. Consistently one of the most anticipated events of the week for the global agency community. Invite-only.

MassiveMusic and Monks co-host a beachside music and networking party on the Croisette, featuring live DJ sets and a gathering of music, production, and advertising professionals. Free/invite depending on access.

Nielsen's morning session on unifying cross-screen audience measurement in 2026. Covers Nielsen ONE — the company's deduplicated cross-platform measurement solution — the industry's push for a common currency for TV and digital, and what unified measurement means for media planning and buying. Invite-only.

Adthrive (now Raptive)'s morning session supporting independent content creators and the creator-led web. Covers ad monetization best practices, how independent publishers can compete with large platforms, and Raptive's tools for growing creator businesses. Invite-only.

A casual community photography walk through Le Suquet — Cannes' charming medieval old town — for festival attendees looking to explore the real Cannes beyond the Croisette. Led by members of the Cannes Photo Club, covering the city's historic architecture, markets, and coastal panoramas. A relaxing, creative break from the festival hustle. Free; open registration.

The List closing breakfast - Ad Age
__4: Ad Age closes out The List programming with a final Thursday morning breakfast gathering at 57 Bd de la Croisette — a send-off for honorees, partners, and community members as the festival enters its final days. Free with registration.

Session:Cultural OS: New brand intelligence for winning with creators & fans
__4: Kantar presents a new framework — the 'Cultural OS' — for understanding how brands can systematically align with creator and fan culture to drive relevance, earned media, and commercial growth. Covers fandom, creator ecosystems, and the brand intelligence needed to win in culture-driven markets. Free with registration.

Tapad's session on unified identity solutions in a fragmented media landscape. Covers Tapad's device graph technology, cross-screen attribution, and how brands can maintain audience continuity and measurement precision across mobile, desktop, tablet, and connected TV. Invite-only.

Campaign US editor Luz Corona leads a roundtable discussion on the 2026 Grand Prix predictions, the year's defining creative trends, and the biggest conversations emerging from Cannes Lions this week. Open to Campaign US community members. Free with registration.

Hearst's invitation-only luxury symposium at the Hotel Majestic — a curated gathering for brand leaders from the luxury sector to discuss the evolving luxury consumer journey, digital media strategy for high-end brands, and the enduring value of premium editorial environments in building luxury brand equity. Invite-only.

StackAdapt's lunch session on multi-channel programmatic advertising strategies. Covers the StackAdapt DSP's capabilities across CTV, display, native, audio, and in-game — with a focus on precision targeting, contextual intelligence, and performance measurement across formats. Invite-only.

Unity's session on advertising in 3D, VR, and gaming environments. Covers Unity's advertising SDK, immersive and rewarded ad formats, the growing gaming audience, and the brand opportunity in mobile, PC, and console gaming powered by the Unity engine. Invite-only.

Xandr (now part of Microsoft Advertising)'s session on the strategic value of premium programmatic inventory — covering addressable TV, programmatic guaranteed deals, and how data-driven media investment in premium environments drives brand performance. Invite-only.

Reddit's live Ask Me Anything event at Reddit Beach — one of the festival's most genuinely interactive sessions. Industry icons and Reddit executives answer live audience questions in real time, bringing the Reddit community's candid spirit to Cannes. Invite-only at Reddit Beach.

Sojern's data-driven travel marketing session, presenting proprietary travel demand signals and audience insights for 2026. Covers destination marketing, hotel advertising, tour and activity sector trends, and how travel brands are using programmatic to reach travelers at critical moments in the booking journey. Invite-only.

The Drum records a live episode of Creative Brief at The Drum Pub — a live recording of the weekly industry podcast/show featuring guests breaking down the festival's biggest creative moments, award trends, and conversation topics from Cannes Lions 2026. Free with registration.

Condé Nast's session on the convergence of Vogue editorial authority and brand commerce — covering influencer marketing, luxury advertising strategies, the premium editorial environment advantage, and how fashion and lifestyle brands are navigating the creator economy. Invite-only.

LiveIntent's yacht session on the enduring commercial power of email as a high-intent, privacy-compliant, first-party data channel. Covers contextual advertising in newsletters, the inbox as a brand-safe ad environment, and why email is experiencing a renaissance in the age of cookie deprecation. Invite-only.

A private afternoon excursion to the vineyards of Provence, organized for festival attendees seeking a genuine escape from the Croisette. Includes a guided tour, wine tasting, and a scenic drive through the French countryside. A rare chance to experience the real French Riviera beyond the festival buzz. Free; pre-registration required.

PubMatic's Thursday session focused on maximizing the value of premium video and CTV inventory — covering programmatic TV buying strategies, private marketplace structures for video, audience quality in streaming, and the measurement frameworks brands need to justify CTV investment. Invite-only.

Magnite's Thursday afternoon cocktail reception on their yacht — one of the festival's top programmatic-focused networking events. Features conversation on the future of CTV advertising, streaming supply dynamics, and Magnite's position as the leading independent sell-side platform. Free/invite depending on access.

IAS's session on verifying the quality of the connected TV advertising experience — covering brand safety, viewability, and fraud measurement specifically in streaming environments, and IAS's roadmap for extending ad verification into new CTV surfaces. Invite-only.

TripleLift's session on the evolution of native ad formats — covering shoppable native, contextual native, and creative native formats across publisher environments. Discusses how native advertising's quality-first approach drives brand engagement and conversion. Invite-only.

DoubleVerify presents their annual update on the digital ad fraud landscape — covering new fraud typologies in CTV and mobile, the latest in invalid traffic detection methodology, and the global state of the digital supply chain integrity. Invite-only.

Live Nation: The Power of Live
__4: Live Nation's cocktail reception and panel on the commercial power of live entertainment sponsorship — featuring sneak peeks at upcoming touring acts and brand partnership opportunities across Live Nation venues, festivals, and stadium events. Covers why live experiences remain among the most powerful brand touchpoints. Invite-only.

Brandwatch's session on tracking the ROI of social conversation and turning consumer intelligence into brand strategy. Covers their AI-powered social listening tools, how brands are using real-time consumer data to inform everything from product development to crisis management. Invite-only.

Exclusive Drinks & Networking Finale: Driving the Future of Marketing
__3: FT x Nikkei Teahouse, 11 Rue de Belges, 06400 Cannes
__4: The Financial Times closes its week of Cannes programming with an invitation-only networking reception at the FT x Nikkei Teahouse — bringing together senior marketing executives for candid conversation about the future of marketing investment, brand accountability, and media strategy. A high-quality send-off for the FT's Cannes community. Invite-only.

Omnicom's invitation-only Thursday networking reception bringing together global leadership and key clients from across the Omnicom network — including BBDO, TBWA, DDB, OMD, PHD, and Hearts & Science — for cocktails and informal networking. Invite-only.

The Influencer Marketing Factory presents new Gen Z trust and influencer engagement data in partnership with eMarketer at the Five Seas Hotel rooftop. For senior brand and agency marketers who need current research to guide influencer investment decisions. Free/invite depending on access.

An exclusive Thursday evening reception at the Majestic Hotel celebrating Clio Award winners at Cannes Lions. Features Clio medallists, jury members, and the broader creative community in a celebration of the week's most recognized work. Invite-only through Clio.

__3: MOKA | 9 Rue du Commandant André, 06400 Cannes
__4: MarkApp and SpinX host a curated sunset cocktail gathering at MOKA (9 Rue du Commandant André). Features carefully crafted cocktails, a selection of elegant canapés, and a relaxed but refined atmosphere in one of Cannes' most stylish venues. Deliberately not a traditional networking event — designed for genuine conversation and meaningful connections among professionals, creatives, and innovators. Free with registration.

LBB & Friends: The Big Beach Party
__4: LBB's legendary Thursday night beach party — the biggest single event at the LBB & Friends Beach and one of the can't-miss social highlights of the entire Cannes Lions week. In 2026, LBB celebrates their 15th year on the beach, making this a special anniversary edition. Features DJ sets, a festive atmosphere, and all the warmth for which LBB Beach is famous. A beloved institution for the global creative community. Free/invite; priority for LBB members and sponsors.

Publicis Groupe's formal Thursday evening executive dinner at a private villa — an invitation-only gathering of senior Publicis leadership and key global clients for formal dining and high-level relationship building. Invite-only.

__3: Vega la Plage, 13 Boulevard de la Croisette, 06400 Cannes
__4: Canva's Pride celebration at Vega la Plage — part of Canva's four-day Cannes Lions activation at the Creative Cabana, which runs all week with talks, AI design demos, a magic-meets-AI variety show, confetti showers, and gelato. The Thursday Pride Party is open to the full Cannes community. Free with registration.

Beats by Dre's exclusive Thursday night beach party featuring live DJ sets, product demonstrations, and Beats merchandise giveaways. A high-energy, music-led evening event that celebrates the intersection of sound culture and brand. Invite-only.

Spotify Beach's closing concert night of the 2026 festival — featuring a surprise headliner performance. The biggest night on Spotify Beach, capping the week's evening programming with a world-class live music experience. Past surprise performers have included global superstars. This is the night everyone talks about the next morning. Invite-only; contact your Spotify representative.

__3: Hôtel Martinez, 73 Bd de la Croisette, 06400 Cannes
__4: The Women Inspiring Network's hosted lounge and gathering space at Hotel Martinez (73 Bd de la Croisette) — a community hub for women in advertising and media throughout the festival week. The Thursday gathering features networking, mentorship conversations, programming on career development and leadership, and a supportive, inclusive community atmosphere. Free/invite depending on session.

A gentle guided meditation session on the Jetée Albert-Edouard — a calming way to center and ground yourself before the final day of the festival and the Grand Prix awards ceremony. A moment of stillness before the big close. Cannes Lions badge required.

The annual launch of Kantar's BrandZ report — one of the most anticipated research releases of the entire Cannes Lions festival. Presents the world's most valuable brands ranking, category trend analysis, regional breakdowns, and the methodology behind brand equity valuation. A must-attend for brand strategists, marketers, and investors. Free with registration.

Acxiom's session on precision marketing through identity resolution and first-party data at the heart of creative strategy. Covers first-party data onboarding, audience extension through clean rooms, and how privacy-compliant identity solutions enable personalization at scale. Invite-only.

Integral Ad Science: Global Media Quality
__4: IAS closes the week with their roadmap for ad verification, brand safety, and viewability standards heading into 2027 — incorporating the latest learning from a full festival of CTV and digital advertising conversations. Invite-only.

TikTok's full Friday activation (11AM–6PM) exploring how music, sound, and trending audio drive content virality and shape global cultural movements on the platform. Features creator sessions, music industry guests, and live demonstrations of TikTok's sound-driven discovery algorithm. Invite-only.

Bazaarvoice presents new research on Gen Z trust and the commercial power of user reviews, in partnership with eMarketer, at the Five Seas Hotel. Covers how authentic consumer content drives purchase decisions across generations and what brands need to know about UGC strategy in 2026. Invite-only.

AdExchanger's closing-day roundup of the week's programmatic and ad tech trends — covering the biggest technology announcements, key themes from across the week's sessions, and an expert panel's predictions for what comes next in the industry. Essential for anyone in the programmatic and ad tech space. Invite-only.

Amobee (now Nexxen)'s session on strategic CTV media planning — covering cross-screen campaign architecture, audience targeting in streaming environments, measurement frameworks for connected TV, and how media agencies are approaching CTV upfront negotiations. Invite-only.

The most important event of the Cannes Lions International Festival of Creativity 2026 — the Friday evening Grand Prix Awards Ceremony at the Palais des Festivals (7PM–9PM). Features the presentation of the most prestigious Lions awards: Grand Prix, Titanium Lions, Glass Lions, and category Grand Prix winners. The culmination of a year of global creative excellence. Black tie optional. Cannes Lions badge required.

The legendary final celebration of Cannes Lions 2026 at Carlton Beach following the Grand Prix ceremony. The entire creative community comes together one last time for DJ sets, drinks, and the euphoria of a week of extraordinary creativity. A Cannes Lions tradition for 70+ years. Cannes Lions badge required.

Google hosts the festival's spectacular closing night fireworks display and reception at the Google Pier — offering one of the best vantage points on the Croisette for the finale. A breathtaking send-off for Cannes Lions 2026. Invite-only through Google.

DanAds, the leading self-serve advertising platform powering major publishers worldwide, joins Cannes Lions 2026 with meetings and programmed sessions. DanAds' technology enables publishers to offer advertisers direct, self-serve access to premium inventory. Full schedule TBC — register interest at their website.

A series of talks, workshops, and informal sessions across the festival week organized by Different Minds, surfacing the topic of neurodiversity in the creative and advertising industries. Covers building inclusive workplaces for neurodiverse talent, the business case for cognitive diversity, and stories from neurodiverse leaders across the industry. Full dates and times TBC — register interest at differentminds.media.

A casual, low-key Saturday brunch at Carlton Beach organized by Cannes Lions for badge holders staying through the weekend. The perfect wind-down after an intense five days — comfortable clothes, good food, and the gentle decompression of a community that survived Cannes together. Cannes Lions badge required.

Daily early-morning networking for sunrise risers at a local bakery on Rue Meynadier — the heart of Cannes' daily market street. Simple, welcoming, informal. A good antidote to jet lag and an easy way to meet fellow attendees before the day begins. Free; no registration required.

A daily guided morning meditation at the Jetée Albert-Edouard for attendees seeking a mental health moment away from the intensity of the festival. Run by a local yoga center; drop in at any point during the week. Free; no registration required.

A daily co-working space on Rue d'Antibes — conveniently located near the Palais — available throughout the festival week for catching up on emails, taking calls, or getting work done. A valuable resource in a city where quiet, Wi-Fi-equipped work spaces are scarce during festival week. Free; no registration required.

MediaLink's legendary invitation-only executive lounge at the Hotel du Cap — located away from the bustle of Cannes in Cap d'Antibes — is one of the most exclusive and productive deal-making environments at the entire festival. A private gathering space for C-suite executives, major investors, platform leaders, and senior industry decision-makers. If you receive an invitation, it supersedes everything else on your calendar. All week, daily 9AM–6PM. Invite-only through MediaLink.

The Wall Street Journal: Tech Terrace
__4: Journal House returns to the Croisette for Cannes Lions 2026 with four days of WSJ programming exploring AI, media accountability, and brand trust. Editor-led interviews, intimate member sessions, and evening gatherings — drawing some of the highest-profile names on the Croisette. Consistently one of the most intellectually rigorous programming tracks at the festival. Free/invite depending on session. Find details at journalhouse.wsj.com.

Uber Ads showcases its expansion into advertising and hospitality across the full festival week at the Uber Hub. Features demonstrations of Uber's advertising solutions across ride-hail, Uber Eats, and Uber for Business — plus sessions on AI-powered logistics, customer experience innovation, and how Uber is evolving into a full-funnel media network. Invite-only.

Fast Company's Villa hosts a full week of interviews and panel discussions with the innovators, entrepreneurs, CMOs, and brand founders shaping the future of business and creativity. All-week programming on technology, creativity, social impact, and the intersection of culture and commerce. Invite-only.

Havas Café's daily morning programming track (9–10:30AM) sets the tone for each festival day with trend briefings, fresh insights from Havas' annual Meaningful Brands research, and structured conversation over breakfast. A welcoming start to the day for Havas network partners and clients. Invite-only.

Little Black Book's LBB & Friends Beach celebrates its 15th year at Cannes Lions in 2026 — one of the longest-running independent fixtures at the festival. A beach restaurant that stays deliberately approachable: no intimidating door policy, no velvet-rope energy. Open daily as a casual gathering space with daily panels, discussion tracks, and spontaneous networking. Daily free drinks (beer, wine, soft drinks) for wristband holders between 4–6PM — arrive early, it gets busy. Headline sponsor: Murphy Cobb and Associates (MCA). Free/invite depending on access level.

Havas' full-week hub at the Havas Café exploring brand purpose and the lessons of their annual Meaningful Brands study. Daily programming covers the commercial value of meaning, the link between sustainability and brand equity, and how brands can create societal value without sacrificing business performance. Invite-only.

Roku Beach is the festival's center for streaming advertising — a full-week activation showcasing Roku's platform reach, OneView ad platform, streaming audience data, and the future of TV advertising in a streaming-first world. Daily programming on CTV strategy, audience addressability, and Roku's content ecosystem. Invite-only.

Dentsu Villa hosts full-week programming at the intersection of data and creativity — covering Dentsu's proprietary AI tools, campaign effectiveness research from Dentsu's Intelligence team, and the principles of brand building in an increasingly fragmented, algorithmically mediated media environment. Invite-only.

NBCU's full-week Terrace activation showcases Peacock's growing content slate, NBCUniversal's One Platform advertising solutions (spanning linear TV, streaming, digital, and live sports), and the future of audience engagement in a streaming-and-live world. Coverage includes Olympics, NFL on NBC, and Peacock exclusives. Invite-only.

Activision Blizzard Media's gaming-centric hub (AB Hub) features live gaming tournaments, brand partnership showcases, and sessions on the advertising opportunity across Call of Duty, World of Warcraft, Candy Crush, and the broader Activision Blizzard ecosystem — now part of Microsoft. Invite-only.

Paramount Beach is the destination for Paramount+ content showcases, film and TV previews, and advertising partnership opportunities. Covers Paramount's global media brands including Paramount+, CBS, MTV, BET, Nickelodeon, and Paramount Pictures — and how brands can reach audiences across Paramount's unified advertising platform. Invite-only.

TikTok's exclusive all-week space for the platform's top-tier creators to network, film content, take meetings with brand marketing teams, and connect with each other in an environment that mirrors TikTok's energy. A lively, creative hub within the TikTok Garden. Invite-only.

YouTube Beach celebrates 21 years of YouTube with a full week of programming on the creator economy, YouTube's advertising solutions, and the platform's continued role as the world's leading long-form video destination. Features creator conversations, brand case studies, and sessions on YouTube's latest ad formats and measurement tools. Invite-only.

L’Oréal: The Future of Beauty Tech
__4: Immersive beauty tech and Gen AI experiences.

Samsung's full-week hub (Samsung Hub) showcases the AI-powered smart home ecosystem and Samsung Ads' advanced targeting capabilities across Samsung Smart TVs, Galaxy devices, and connected home products. Features live demos of Samsung's advertising solutions and sessions on reaching the connected household. Free with registration (pre-registration required via Cvent link).

Disney's invitation-only Hub showcases the unified Disney advertising ecosystem spanning Disney+, Hulu, ESPN+, ABC, and Freeform — and how brands can reach audiences across Disney's premium content portfolio through Disney's unified advertising solutions. Features sessions on sports marketing (ESPN), streaming, and family content strategy. Invite-only.

Google Pier: The AI Creative Lab
__4: Google's full-week AI Creative Lab at the Google Pier offers creative professionals hands-on access to cutting-edge AI tools — including Gemini AI integrations for creative production, Google DeepMind demos, AI-assisted video and image generation, and Google's latest innovations for marketers. Open daily 10AM–5PM. A must-visit for anyone interested in where AI meets creativity. Invite-only.

Omnicom's full-week suite at Cannes brings together the data science, analytics, and creative capabilities of the Omnicom network to demonstrate Creative Intelligence — the company's approach to combining data-driven insights with world-class creative execution. Features sessions from BBDO, TBWA, DDB, and Omnicom Media Group. Invite-only.

Apple's exclusive full-week Villa sessions for senior creative professionals — focusing on Apple's creative tools ecosystem, including Final Cut Pro, Logic Pro, Motion, and Apple hardware in professional production workflows. Covers Apple's role in the future of creative production for advertising and content. Invite-only.

A free, open cultural exhibition celebrating local Cannes and French Riviera artists at the Hotel de Ville. A genuinely refreshing alternative to the festival's commercial programming — featuring painting, photography, and mixed media from the local artist community. Open all week, 11AM–6PM. Free; no registration required.

A daily guided historical tour of Le Suquet — Cannes' beautiful medieval old quarter — offered throughout the festival week for attendees who want to experience the real Cannes beyond the Croisette. Features the city's 11th-century castle, Notre Dame de l'Espérance church, panoramic views over the Bay of Cannes, and the vibrant Forville market. Led by local Cannes Tourism guides. Free; no registration required.

3C Ventures operates Plage 3CV (Lucia Beach) as a premium full-week networking venue at the intersection of venture capital, advertising, and marketing technology. A gathering space for investors, founders, adtech and martech entrepreneurs, and senior marketing leaders exploring the future of the industry from a business and investment lens. Free with registration.

__3: Majestic Hotel, 10 Boulevard de la Croisette
__4: Adobe's comprehensive full-week presence at Cannes Lions 2026 spans multiple activations: official headline partnership of LIONS Creators Beach (daily 9AM–6PM in front of the Palais), executive meetings hub at the Majestic Hotel (10 Boulevard de la Croisette), exclusive sponsorship of the Young Lions Competition, and panel appearances across the Croisette. Adobe is positioned as the platform at the center of creativity, marketing, and AI innovation at the 2026 festival. Free with registration.

Adelaide Metrics' full-week presence at Cannes Lions, including the Monday breakfast, AU Après Dark party, and ongoing sessions on Attention Quality measurement — Adelaide's proprietary metric for evaluating media environments based on their ability to drive genuine attention. A key talking point for media planners at the 2026 festival. Free with registration.

PMG's AI and Technology Sandbox debuts at Miramar Beach — taking over the space formerly occupied by WPP Beach. PMG positions itself as a technology company that happens to do marketing, and the Sandbox reflects that: expect AI hackathons, startup showcases, developer demonstrations, and programming on data-driven marketing innovation. A tech-first alternative to traditional agency beach activations. Free with registration.

The Brands & Culture Villa at 13 Avenue de Benefiat (a 16-minute walk from the Palais, 8 minutes from the beach) operates as a more intimate, culturally-rooted alternative to the beachfront mainstream. A full-week programme centered on the intersection of brand strategy and culture — with a particular emphasis on multicultural marketing, brand anthropology, and the role of cultural relevance in brand growth. Free with registration.

Beet.tv's full-week video interview and content programming at Hotel Barrière Le Majestic (10 Bd de la Croisette). Beet.tv is the premier video journalism platform for the digital media and advertising industry; their Cannes sessions capture executive perspectives from the festival's most significant events and make them available on-demand. The 2026 edition also celebrates Beet.tv's 20th anniversary. Free with registration.

__3: Vega la Plage, 13 Boulevard de la Croisette, 06400 Cannes
__4: Canva's Creative Cabana at Vega la Plage runs all week with a packed four-day program: AI-powered design tool talks and demos, a magic-meets-AI variety show, a Pride party (Thursday), confetti showers, and gelato. The Cabana brings Canva's accessible, creativity-for-everyone ethos to the Croisette — an energetic, fun destination for creatives at all levels. Free with registration.

The official LIONS Creators Beach — now in its most prominent location yet, at the Parvis in front of the Palais des Festivals — is the dedicated hub for the creator economy at Cannes Lions 2026.
