Powered by Adobe as official headline partner, the Beach offers an exclusive stage, content studio, editing suite, meeting areas, and activations all centred on the business of influence. Running daily 9AM–6PM, June 22–26, with a full programme of creator-focused talks, brand-creator networking, and sessions featuring speakers including Mel Robbins. The move to beach level (from the Palais rooftop) reflects the creator economy's central role in modern marketing. LIONS Creators Pass required for some sessions; general badge holders can access much of the open programming.

DEPT's Secret Garden returns for 2026 at 5 Rdpt Duboys d'Angers (just off the Croisette), running June 23–25. Designed as a deliberate antidote to the noise and transactional nature of Cannes, the Garden filled up fast in past years and this year is by invitation. Features real conversations from brand and industry leaders on AI-native workflows, discoverability in algorithmic environments, and the evolving nature of creative craft — with live demos of Deptify® (DEPT's AI platform), a curated Souvenir Shop (daily 12–1PM), and intimate evening dinners. Programming includes Fortune CMO roundtables, Oxford Saïd Business School sessions, and discussions with Microsoft and the Ad Council. Register interest at deptagency.com.

Digiday's full week of Cannes programming includes: the Cannes Brand Leaders Dinner (Monday, invite-only), the Cannes Marketing Leaders Brunch (Tuesday, invite-only), a Panel & Cocktail Reception (Tuesday, application-required), and ongoing editorial coverage and activations across the week. Digiday's events consistently deliver some of the festival's most substantive, senior media and marketing conversations. Free/invite depending on event.

__3: Nikkei FT Teahouse, 11 Rue des Belges, 06400 Cannes
__4: The Financial Times' comprehensive full-week presence at the FT x Nikkei Teahouse (11 Rue des Belges) encompasses four major events: the Ad Council Brunch (June 23), the FT x People Like Us Evening on Inclusion (June 23), the FT Morning Insights Brunch — The Business of Influence (June 24), and the Driving the Future of Marketing Drinks Finale (June 25). One of the festival's most intellectually rigorous and prestigious programming tracks. Invite-only.

__3: Hôtel Martinez, 73 Bd de la Croisette, 06400 Cannes
__4: The Female Quotient's Empower Café returns to Cannes Lions 2026 as an inclusive hub for bold conversations, meaningful connections, and fresh perspectives on the future of creativity and leadership. Located at the Hotel Martinez (73 Bd de la Croisette), The FQ's spaces range from penthouse sessions to beachfront gatherings — designed for those who come to Cannes to build the meaningful relationships that drive business forward. All are welcome. Free with registration.

__3: Mondrian Hotel, located at 45 Bd de la Croisette, 06400 Cannes
__4: Infillion's full-week presence at the Mondrian Hotel (45 Bd de la Croisette) offers a programme of social events, creative content sessions, and demonstrations of Infillion's engagement advertising formats — including TrueX, interactive video, and attention-based ad products. Features an invitation to raise a glass to another year of digital creativity. Free/invite depending on event.

Inkwell Beach returns for Cannes Lions 2026 — a beloved community gathering space known for its diverse, inclusive atmosphere and genuine warmth. Open to all who register, Inkwell Beach is a dedicated space celebrating diverse voices in the advertising and marketing industry. Free with registration.

__3: Fervor, 5 Rue des Frères Casanova, 06400 Cannes
__4: MSQ's Le Club MSQ at Fervor (5 Rue des Frères Casanova) operates as a private member space on June 23–24, hosting: the Ritualicious Lunch (Wednesday, invite-only), the 'Last Night a DJ Saved My Pitch' DJ/talk evening (Wednesday, free with registration), and intimate Secret Garden Dinner evenings. A stylish, hospitality-forward MSQ partner venue off the main Croisette drag. Free/invite depending on event.

__3: Les Monks Café, 5 Sq. Mérimée,  06400 Cannes
__4: Monks' dedicated Cannes space at 5 Sq. Mérimée operates throughout the festival week as a hospitality and meeting venue for Monks clients and creative partners. A lower-key, less chaotic alternative to beachside activations — ideal for client meetings, creative conversations, and informal networking in the Monks creative community. Free/invite depending on access.

The inaugural LIONS Sport programme at Cannes Lions runs June 24–25 at Carlton Hotel in partnership with SPORT BEACH — a new two-day programme dedicated to the rapidly evolving business of sports marketing. Pass holders access all LIONS Sport talks and sessions, SPORT BEACH networking at Plage du Festival (June 21–25), the Entertainment Lions for Sport Awards Gala (Wednesday evening), and curated networking with sports marketing leaders. Key themes: Storytelling as the New Sports Currency, Athletes as Cultural Architects and Owners, The Shifting Economics of Sports, Community as Infrastructure for Modern Fandom, and The Globalization of Sports Brands. Featured speakers include Hilary Knight (2x Olympic Gold, Team USA Hockey), Britt Wickes (Tennis Australia), Brandon Snow (RedBird Capital Partners), and leaders from Boardroom, Vevo, and Sweatwork. Requires a paid LIONS Sport Pass.

Maison NDA at 59 Rue Félix Faure (Duke's 59) is a multi-space venue operating throughout festival week, just minutes from the Palais. Features private dining rooms, versatile meeting spaces, vibrant outdoor terraces, and exclusive bars — hosting a rotating programme of industry events including the Publisher Perspectives Happy Hour and Affiliate/Commerce Happy Hour. A premium independent venue in the heart of Cannes. Free/invite depending on event.

__3: Plage Rado Beach, Armani Café, LVMH Villa, Cannes
__4: Brand Innovators' full-week Marketing Leadership Summit at multiple premium Cannes venues (Plage Rado Beach, Armani Café, LVMH Villa) bills itself as the most CMO-dense programming track at the festival. Expect A-list chief marketing officers, global brand leaders, and the world's most influential marketers sharing insights on the future of creativity and brand-building. Consistently draws the most senior brand-side attendees of any event at Cannes. Free with registration.

OnAudience @ Cannes - Exclusive Yacht Briefing for Leaders Shaping the Future of AdTech
__3: Port de Cannes -> Golfe de la Napoule
__4: OnAudience hosts exclusive yacht briefings for senior adtech leaders — sailing from Port de Cannes to the Golfe de la Napoule. An intimate, completely private environment for high-level conversation on audience data, programmatic innovation, and the future of adtech. Invite-only.

RTL's full-week activation at CBeach (Boulevard de la Croisette) features daily programming on European media, pan-continental brand advertising, and RTL's Ad Alliance — one of Europe's largest cross-media advertising networks spanning broadcast, streaming, and digital. Particularly relevant for brands with significant European presence. Free/invite depending on access.

Ladies Who Strategize operates a dedicated full-week salon space (7 minutes from the Palais, 8 minutes from the beach) throughout Cannes Lions 2026 — providing a community hub for senior women in advertising and marketing strategy. In addition to the Monday morning breakfast, the Salon hosts ongoing conversations, informal meetups, and curated programming throughout the week. A much-needed community space for women navigating a busy festival week. Free with registration.
